summary: Tested admin panel single-password auth (gustavo2026) end-to-end. Backend: 14/14 pytest cases pass — admin login (correct/wrong), JWT verify (none/invalid/valid), brute-force lockout after 5 failures (429 + 'Muitas tentativas'), protected PUT /api/stats and GET /api/leads, public GET /api/stats and POST /api/leads, no _id leak anywhere. Frontend: /admin/login renders with correct testids, wrong password shows red 'Senha incorreta' error, /admin without token redirects to /admin/login, correct password navigates to panel and stores gb_admin_token in localStorage, sites_delivered edit + save persists to backend (verified via /api/stats=17), niche add/remove works, green success notice appears, logout clears token and redirects, landing page (/) still loads.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:124-133 — check_lockout() has a redundant 'count >= MAX_ATTEMPTS' fallback branch. Once register_failure sets locked_until at count==5, the locked_until check above already catches it; the second branch is only hit if locked_until somehow wasn't set. Harmless but dead code on the happy path.
server.py:199 — hmac.compare_digest is good (constant-time compare). Good practice.

server.py:239-245 — CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is invalid per the CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Currently OK because the frontend uses Bearer tokens (not cookies), but if cookies are ever added this must be tightened to an explicit origin list.
server.py:254-257 — startup_event uses deprecated FastAPI on_event; consider migrating to lifespan context manager (non-blocking).
AdminLogin.jsx:30 — error display reads err.response?.data?.detail, which on 429 lockout is the Portuguese 'Muitas tentativas' string — handled correctly via formatApiError.
AdminPanel.jsx:182 — sitesDelivered stored as string from input; converted via Number() at save time. Works but allows visually-empty input; backend coerces to max(0, int()) so safe.
JWT_SECRET committed to .env in repo — acceptable for preview env, must be rotated and stored in secret manager for production.
No rate limit on POST /api/leads — public endpoint could be spammed. Out of scope for this iteration but worth noting.
test_credentials: Admin password: gustavo2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and ADMIN_PASSWORD in /app/backend/.env)
seed_data_creation: None. Test cleans up its own login_attempts collection (via pymongo) before/after the brute-force test. sites_delivered restored to 10 after frontend test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 14 pytest cases at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py cover full admin auth flow + brute-force. The brute-force test resets the login_attempts collection before AND after running so it does not poison other runs. If you change ADMIN_PASSWORD, update the ADMIN_PASSWORD constant at the top of backend_test.py. Iframed Netlify portfolio sites emit a 'createAxiosClient is not defined' console error — this is third-party noise from /portfolio iframes, not this app.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All 14 backend tests + 8 frontend flow checks pass.
